content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

April 21, 1999

Gloria White-Brown
South Coast Air Quality Management District
Post Office Box 4940
Diamond Bar, California 91765-0940

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-99-073

Dear Ms. White-Brown:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

QUESTIONS

1. Would distribution of a newsletter containing biographical information about two new
district board members of the South Coast Air Quality Management District ("district"), who are
also elected officials, be in violation of the mass mailing provisions?

2. If the elected officials review the article in the newsletter for accuracy before being
distributed, would that be considered a violation of the mass mailing provisions?

3. When an agency gets a new appointed board member, how should the agency inform
the public?

CONCLUSIONS

1. If the mailing is not being prepared or sent in cooperation, consultation, coordination,
or concert with the elected officers, use of the elected officers' names is permitted provided the
officers are not featured in the mailing.

___________________________

¹ Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109 -
18995, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. A-99-073
Page 2

2. If the two newly elected officers review the newsletter for accuracy, the newsletter
would be prepared or sent in cooperation, consultation, coordination, or concert with the elected
officials.

3. As long as the newsletter does not meet the four prongs of the mass mailing
prohibition, the district would not be prohibited from producing and sending the mailing.
However, if the mass mailing meets the criteria listed in Regulation 18901(a), the district would
need to refer to the exceptions in Regulation 18901 as an acceptable means of notifying the
public regarding any newly appointed directors to the board, who are elected officials, i.e, press
releases, etc.

FACTS

The South Coast Air Quality Management District ("district") is a regional governmental
agency encompassing the counties of Los Angeles, Orange, Riverside and the non-desert portions
of San Bernardino County. A board consisting of twelve appointed members governs the
district. You explained to me in our conversation of April 13, 1999, that three of the board
members are appointed officials. The remaining nine officials are elected officers who are
appointed to the board pursuant to Health and Safety Code Section 40420 et seq.

Health and Safety Code Section 40420 et seq. provides that one member must be
appointed by the Governor; one member must be appointed by the Senate Committee on Rules;
one member must be appointed by the Speaker of the Assembly; four members must be
appointed by the boards of supervisors of the counties in the south coast district; three members
must be appointed by cities in the south coast district; one member must be appointed by the
cities of the western region of Los Angeles County; and one member must be appointed by the
cities of the eastern region of Los Angeles County.

The district would like to publish and distribute a newsletter. The newsletter would
contain an article including biographical information about two new district board members. The
article would also include other information of public interest regarding the two board members.
A copy of the proposed newsletter was attached to your incoming advice request. The district is
producing and sending the mailing. The costs of distribution are paid for with public monies.
The newsletters are sent unsolicited. More than 200 similar newsletters are sent in a single
calendar month to over 200 recipients. You also called me on March 26, 1999, and asked me to
add Question No. 3 to your advice request.

ANALYSIS

Section 89001 states:

"No newsletter or other mass mailing shall be sent at public expense."

File No. A-99-073
Page 3

Regulation 18901 (copy enclosed) interprets Section 89001:

"(a) Except as provided in subdivision (b), a mailing is prohibited by section
89001 if all of the following criteria are met:
(1) Any item sent is delivered, by any means, to the recipient at his or her
residence, place of employment or business, or post office box. For purposes of
this subdivision (a)(1), the item delivered to the recipient must be a tangible item,
such as a videotape, record, or button, or a written document.
(2) The item sent either:
(A) Features an elected officer affiliated with the agency which produces or
sends the mailing, or
(B) Includes the name, office, photograph, or other reference to an elected
officer affiliated with the agency which produces or sends the mailing, and is
prepared or sent in cooperation, consultation, coordination, or concert with the
elected officer.
(3)(A) Any of the costs of distribution is paid for with public moneys; or
(B) Costs of design, production, and printing exceeding $50.00 are paid with
public moneys, and the design, production, or printing is done with the intent of
sending the item other than as permitted by this regulation.
(4) More than two hundred substantially similar items are sent, in a single
calendar month, excluding any item sent in response to an unsolicited request and
any item described in subdivision (b).
(b) [provides exceptions to the general rule.]" (Emphasis added.)

Subdivision (c)(1) of Regulation 18901 defines the term "elected officer affiliated with
the agency" as follows:

"(1) 'Elected officer affiliated with the agency' means an elected officer who is
a member, officer, or employee of the agency, or of a subnit thereof such as a
committee, or who has supervisory control over the agency, or who appoints one
or more members of the agency." (Emphasis added.)

An elected officer is affiliated with an agency if the officer is a member, officer, or
employee of the agency or a subnit (such as a committee) of that agency; has supervisory
control over the agency; or appoints any of the members of the agency. (Regulation 18901(c)(1.)
As members of the district, the elected officials who are members of the district are affiliated
with the entity that is sending the mailing. (Giattina Advice Letter, No. A-96-201.)

However, the analysis does not end with a determination of affiliation. It must also be
determined whether the mailing is prepared or sent in cooperation, consultation, coordination, or
concert with the elected officer. If the mailing is being prepared or sent in cooperation,
consultation, coordination, or concert with the elected officer, any use of the elected officer's

File No. A-99-073
Page 4

name, photograph or office, or any reference to the officer is prohibited. (Regulation
18901(a)(2)(B).) If the elected officers review for accuracy the article in the newsletter or
provide information for the article, that would be considered cooperating, consulting,
coordinating, or preparing and sending in concert with the elected officer. (Lofgren Advice
Letter, No. I-98-305.)

If the mailing is not being prepared or sent in cooperation, consultation, coordination, or
concert with the elected officers, use of the elected officers' names is permitted provided the
officers are not featured in the mailing. (Regulation 18901(a)(2)(A).) An elected officer is
featured in a mass mailing if he or she is singled out for attention of the reader by use of his or
her signature, inclusion of any photograph, or the manner of display of his or her name or office
in the layout of the document such as by headlines, type size, or typeface. (Regulation
18901(c)(2).) The draft newsletter that you sent with your advice request does not feature the
elected officers' names in the headline or
caption. The type size, typeface, and type color do not vary. Therefore, if the mailing is
prepared and sent without the cooperation, consultation, coordination, or concert with the elected
officers, it would not be a prohibited mass mailing. Please keep in mind that this conclusion is
based on the facts presented to us. It may be possible that a biography in a newsletter could be
construed to feature an elected officer. This determination is made on a case-by-case basis.

Exceptions

If all criteria of subdivision (a) of Regulation 18901 are satisfied, an exception must
apply. There are exceptions provided for the inclusion of an elected officer's name in certain
types of mailings under certain specified circumstances. Please refer to Regulation 18901 for an
itemization of the exceptions. Moreover, it is a fundamental rule of statutory construction that an
exception to a general rule is to be narrowly construed. (Julius Goldman's Egg City v. Air
Pollution Control Dept. of Ventura County (1981) 116 Cal.App.3d 741; Valdez v. Federal Mut.
Ins. Co. (1969) 272 Cal.App.2d 223.)

If you have any questions regarding the above, please feel free to contact me at (916)
322-5660.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: Lynda Doherty
Political Reform Consultant, Legal Division

Section 89001 states:
“No newsletter or other mass mailing shall be sent at publicexpense.”
Regulation 18901 (copy enclosed) interprets Section 89001:
“(a) Except as provided in subdivision (b), a mailing is prohibited by section
89001 if all of the following criteria are met:
(1) Any item sent is delivered, by any means, to the recipient at his or her
residence, place of employment or business, or post office box. For purposes of

such as a videotape, record, or button, or a written document.
(2) The item sent either:

sends the mailing, or

officer affiliated with the agency which produces or sends the mailing, and is
prepared or sent in cooperation, consultation, coordination, or concert with the
elected officer.
(3)(A) Any of the costs of distribution is paid for with public moneys; or
(B) Costs of design, production, and printing exceeding $50.00 are paid with
public moneys, and the design, production, or printing is done with the intent of
sending the item other than as permitted by this regulation.
(4) More than two hundred substantially similar items are sent, in a single
calendar month, excluding any item sent in response to an unsolicited request and
any item described in subdivision (b).
(b) [provides exceptions to the general rule.].” (Emphasis added.)
Subdivision (c)(1) of Regulation 18901 defines the term “elected officer affiliated with

“(1) ‘Elected officer affiliated with the agency’ means an elected officer who is

committee, or who has supervisory control over the agency, or who appoints one

in the layout of the document such as by headlines, type size, or typeface. (Regulation
